skill.name: semantic-code-hunter
skill.description: Use when you need to find code by concept (not just text). Uses Serena MCP for semantic code search across the codebase with minimal token usage. Ideal for understanding architecture, finding authentication flows, or multi-file refactoring.
skill.summary: Find code by concept with Serena MCP semantic search.
skill.author: Barnhardt-Enterprises-Inc

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many backtick-formatted Serena tool examples in SKILL.md as external command execution, but these are documentation examples and tool names, not executable scripts. The weak cryptography finding at SKILL.md:3 is a false positive because the line is a natural-language description and contains no cryptographic algorithm use. The only minor risk is guidance to run the Serena indexing command, which is expected for this skill and does not include user-controlled shell interpolation.

low_findings.title: Serena Indexing Command Guidance
low_findings.description: The skill tells users to run the Serena project indexing command during troubleshooting. This is expected setup guidance for semantic search, but it still asks the agent or user to invoke an external CLI command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain direct command guidance for Serena indexing. The command is fixed and expected for the tool, so the security concern is minor rather than high severity.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Execution Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static external command findings on Serena tool names and examples are documentation-only references. They do not define scripts, execute shell commands, or interpolate user-controlled input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is contained in Markdown lists and fenced examples describing Serena MCP operations. No executable code or shell syntax is present in these locations.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is A False Positive
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography finding at SKILL.md:3 does not correspond to cryptographic code. The line is a plain-language skill description about semantic code search.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains only Markdown frontmatter text and no hash, cipher, key, or cryptographic operation. There is no evidence of weak cryptographic algorithm use.

dangerous_patterns.title: External CLI Command Reference
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill references a fixed Serena indexing command for troubleshooting. This should be run only in trusted repositories and with the expected Serena MCP tooling installed.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command reference is explicit, but it is fixed setup guidance rather than dynamic command execution. The residual concern is environmental trust.

content.value_statement: Large repositories make it hard to find the right implementation path from plain text search alone. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to use Serena MCP semantic tools for targeted code discovery.
actual_capabilities: Guides semantic symbol lookup with Serena MCP tools.
Shows how to trace references between functions, classes, and components.
Supports authentication flow and data flow discovery.
Helps plan multi-file refactoring by finding symbol usages.
Recommends when to combine semantic search with grep or file reads.
limitations: Requires Serena MCP availability and a properly indexed project.
Does not include executable automation or standalone scripts.
Works best when language server support exists for the repository language.
Simple text searches may be faster with grep.

use_cases.title: Trace authentication logic
use_cases.description: Find authentication functions, token validation, and related callers without reading full files.

use_cases.title: Plan safe refactors
use_cases.description: Locate a model or component and identify references before changing names or interfaces.

use_cases.title: Understand unfamiliar architecture
use_cases.description: Map component relationships and data flow in a repository before making changes.

prompt_templates.title: Find a feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Use semantic-code-hunter to find where this repository handles [feature or concept]. Start broad, then list the most relevant symbols and files.

prompt_templates.title: Trace callers
prompt_templates.scenario: Follow usage of a known symbol
prompt_templates.prompt: Use semantic-code-hunter to trace references to [symbol name]. Explain the main call paths and identify files that need review.

prompt_templates.title: Map data flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Understand how a component or service receives data
prompt_templates.prompt: Use semantic-code-hunter to map how [component or service] gets its data. Start with symbol lookup, then trace references back to the source.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a refactor
prompt_templates.prompt: Use semantic-code-hunter to assess a refactor from [old symbol or concept] to [new symbol or concept]. Find definitions, references, related tests, and likely update order.

output_examples.output: The response lists authentication-related symbols, their files, key callers, and a short flow from route handling to token generation.
The response identifies the component definition, parent usage locations, and the upstream API or loader that provides the data.

output_examples.input: What would be affected if User became Account?
output_examples.output: The response summarizes definitions, references, tests, and a suggested review sequence for the rename.

best_practices: Start with broad symbol searches, then narrow by type or file.
Use reference tracing before editing shared symbols.
Fall back to grep for exact string matches and configuration files.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for a single known file when a direct read is enough.
Do not assume semantic results are complete if the project is not indexed.
Do not perform refactors before reviewing references and related tests.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps find code by concept, symbol, and relationships across a repository using Serena MCP guidance.

faq.question: Does it run code automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill is Markdown guidance and does not include executable scripts.

faq.question: Why does it mention grep?
faq.answer: Grep is recommended for simple text searches where semantic search is unnecessary.

faq.question: Does it require Serena MCP?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented workflow assumes Serena MCP tools are available and the project is indexed.

faq.question: Can it help with refactoring?
faq.answer: Yes. It helps find definitions and references so a refactor can be planned before edits begin.

faq.question: Is it useful for small projects?
faq.answer: Usually no. The skill itself recommends grep or direct file reading for small or obvious searches.
